Continual learning extension

Continual learning is not identical to dynamic-capacity learning. It is the sequential-training problem setting. Dynamic-capacity methods form one structural solution branch inside that setting.

Core relationship

Dynamic-capacity learning asks: how should the model structure change during training?

Continual learning asks: how can a model learn sequentially without forgetting old knowledge?

The strongest overlap appears when forgetting is handled by expansion, freezing, task-specific masks, modular routing, pruning-and-reuse, or sparse subnetwork allocation.

Approximate overlap by family

Expansion / columns
very high
Masks / isolation
high
Grow + prune
medium
Sparse rewiring
medium
Replay / regularization
low

Different development path

Early rootCatastrophic interference and the stability-plasticity dilemma define the basic problem: learn new information without overwriting old information.
2016–2018Deep continual learning separates into replay, regularization, distillation, and architectural isolation. Progressive Networks, PathNet, DEN, PackNet, EWC, LwF, iCaRL, GEM, and generative replay become key reference points.
Later phaseEvaluation becomes more protocol-driven: task-incremental, domain-incremental, and class-incremental learning. Structural methods remain important, but replay and distillation dominate many class-incremental settings.
Current linkDynamic-capacity ideas re-enter through expandable representations, sparse task subnetworks, adapters, prompts, routing, and modular systems.

Reading structure for a literature review

A practical flow from the neurogenesis anchor toward broader dynamic-architecture literature.

Recommended narrative

  • Start: Neurogenesis Deep Learning as representation-bottleneck-driven growth.
  • Historical root: constructive neural networks such as Cascade-Correlation.
  • Continual learning: Progressive Networks, DEN, DER, RCL, Learn to Grow.
  • Modern growth: GradMax, NORTH, SENN, MixtureGrowth.
  • Removal: magnitude pruning, Network Slimming, L0 gates, Dynamic Network Surgery.
  • Grow-prune: NeST, MorphNet, structured continuous sparsification.
  • Topology rewiring: SET, RigL, SRigL.
  • Architecture morphism: Net2Net and network-transformation NAS.
Interpretive core: Neurogenesis Deep Learning is best treated as an early deep-learning instance of local capacity growth. Later work generalizes the problem into bottleneck detection, function-preserving expansion, continual-learning protection, and resource-aware removal.
